jokes.text: If this was a cartoon, you'd be looking like a ham right about now.
jokes.setup: They've been waiting for service for half an hour
jokes.punchline: Cartoon reference to being cooked from waiting so long
jokes.explanation: Clever visual metaphor with good economy, though not groundbreaking

jokes.text: Phoebe appearing as their waitress when they call for service
jokes.setup: They're calling for a waitress at a restaurant
jokes.punchline: Their friend Phoebe appears as the waitress
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom coincidence setup, well-executed but predictable

jokes.text: I was over there... then you said, 'Excuse me, miss.' So now I'm here.
jokes.setup: Joey asks what she's doing here
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's literal interpretation of the question
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - literal interpretation masquerading as helpful

jokes.text: Because it's close to where I live, and the aprons are really cute.
jokes.setup: Asked why she's working there
jokes.punchline: Prioritizing cute aprons as job criteria
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Phoebe priorities - superficial concerns treated seriously

jokes.text: Phoebe literally walking away when they ask to 'start over'
jokes.setup: Chandler says 'Can we start over?'
jokes.punchline: Phoebe takes it literally and walks back to start position
jokes.explanation: Excellent physical comedy based on Phoebe's literal mindset - visual punchline

jokes.text: My monkey is out of control.
jokes.setup: Ross discussing behavioral problems
jokes.punchline: The subject is a monkey, not a child
jokes.explanation: Absurd premise treated with parental seriousness - good misdirection

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, I've done that.
jokes.setup: Ross mentions Marcel erasing messages 'by accident'
jokes.punchline: Joey relates to accidentally erasing messages
jokes.explanation: Simple character beat - Joey's relatability to monkey behavior

jokes.text: I've never done that.
jokes.setup: Ross describes monkey peeing on crossword
jokes.punchline: Joey draws the line at this behavior
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Joey relates to some monkey behavior but not others

jokes.text: They're twins. / I don't care. Phoebe's Phoebe. Ursula's hot.
jokes.setup: Chandler insists identical twins look the same
jokes.punchline: Joey's shallow distinction based on attraction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - attraction overrides physical reality

jokes.text: You know that thing when we talk about things? Let's not do that anymore.
jokes.setup: Conversation getting uncomfortable
jokes.punchline: Joey's aversion to meaningful discussion
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about conversation itself - very Joey avoidance of depth

jokes.text: Look, this could be a really long game.
jokes.setup: Wrong guesses about who they saw continue
jokes.punchline: Joey preemptively ends the guessing game
jokes.explanation: Practical Joey cutting through the bit for efficiency

jokes.text: So, um, is she fat?
jokes.setup: Learning about her sister after years apart
jokes.punchline: First question is about weight
jokes.explanation: Awkward honesty about sibling rivalry priorities

jokes.text: Not from where I was standing. / Where were you standing?
jokes.setup: Joey reports on Ursula's appearance
jokes.punchline: Chandler's implication about Joey's viewing angle
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual innuendo setup, well-timed but not original

jokes.text: She was the first to walk... even though I did it later that same day.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining sister's advantages
jokes.punchline: Minimizing the achievement gap to hours
jokes.explanation: Great sibling rivalry logic - technically correct but missing the point

jokes.text: To my parents, by then it was, 'Yeah, what else is new?'
jokes.setup: Phoebe walked the same day as Ursula
jokes.punchline: Parents' dismissive reaction to second child milestone
jokes.explanation: Relatable second child experience with good parental voice

jokes.text: Oh, and I've got earth science, but I'll catch you in gym?
jokes.setup: Ross mentions Lamaze class
jokes.punchline: Phoebe responds as if they're in high school
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's non-sequitur creates alternate reality where they're students

jokes.text: We've got dads. We've got lesbians. The whole parenting team.
jokes.setup: Explaining his complicated family situation
jokes.punchline: Treating diverse family as complete sports roster
jokes.explanation: Sports metaphor for modern family structure - optimistic Ross spin

jokes.text: Ross, that's my jacket. / I know.
jokes.setup: Ross claims to be comfortable with situation
jokes.punchline: Immediately revealed he's wearing wrong jacket
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual contradiction of Ross's claim to be 'comfortable'

jokes.text: Good for you. Alrighty, next?
jokes.setup: Overeager couple announces twin genders
jokes.punchline: Teacher's dismissive, rushed response
jokes.explanation: Professional disinterest in oversharing - realistic but mild

jokes.text: Ross's mumbled explanation of Susan
jokes.setup: Forced to explain complicated relationship
jokes.punchline: Incoherent mumbling instead of clear explanation
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy of awkwardness - Ross avoiding difficult truth

jokes.text: Like buddies. / Like lovers. / You know how close women can get.
jokes.setup: Ross euphemistically describing their relationship
jokes.punchline: Susan's blunt correction, Ross's continued evasion
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Ross's discomfort with honesty

jokes.text: So, twins. Ha. That's like two births. Ouch.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to change subject from their relationship
jokes.punchline: Awkward observation about twins followed by weak reaction
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad small talk showing Ross's social awkwardness

jokes.text: Chandler mimicking intercom buzz
jokes.setup: Intercom buzzes
jokes.punchline: Chandler mimics the sound
jokes.explanation: Simple mimicry gag - shows Chandler's playfulness but low craft

jokes.text: Well, it throws my WENUS out of whack.
jokes.setup: Business discussion about data problems
jokes.punchline: Acronym that sounds like crude anatomy
jokes.explanation: Classic Friends wordplay - professional acronym with sexual undertones

jokes.text: Won't happen again. I wouldn't want to do anything to hurt your... WENUS.
jokes.setup: Nina understanding the acronym meaning
jokes.punchline: Deliberate pause before repeating suggestive word
jokes.explanation: Good callback with sexual tension - Nina playing with the wordplay

jokes.text: You don't dip your pen in the company ink.
jokes.setup: Chandler attracted to coworker
jokes.punchline: Joey's mangled version of 'don't fish off the company pier'
jokes.explanation: Joey's mixed metaphor creates new saying that's actually better

jokes.text: Oh, cool. Urkel in Spanish is Urkel.
jokes.setup: TV switched to Spanish channel
jokes.punchline: Joey's fascination with character names in translation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey observation - finding wonder in mundane cultural details

jokes.text: Well, someone was supposed to write: 'Rach, take down the lights,' and put it on the refrigera...
jokes.setup: Rachel defending why lights are still up
jokes.punchline: Discovers the note on fridge mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - argument demolished by visual evidence

jokes.text: She brought me a tuna melt and four plates of curly fries.
jokes.setup: Joey thinks waitress likes him
jokes.punchline: Excessive food delivery as evidence of attraction
jokes.explanation: Joey's food-based logic for romance - perfectly in character

jokes.text: Score.
jokes.setup: Joey got four plates of fries
jokes.punchline: Chandler's enthusiastic approval of free food
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - Chandler's priorities align with Joey's

jokes.text: So that if we went out on a date, she'd be there.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asks why he wants to date Ursula
jokes.punchline: Joey's literal interpretation of dating purpose
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Joey logic - reducing dating to its most basic function

jokes.text: I mean, it's true, we were one egg once.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining her relationship with her twin
jokes.punchline: Biological fact presented as emotional connection
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's unique perspective on twin biology as relationship status

jokes.text: Do you wanna watch Laverne y Shirley?
jokes.setup: Ross asking Phoebe if she's okay
jokes.punchline: Spanish TV as comfort viewing
jokes.explanation: Mild callback to language change - attempts comfort through familiar show

jokes.text: I'm gonna play my sperm card one more time.
jokes.setup: Competing with Susan for Lamaze coaching role
jokes.punchline: Ross's biological argument for parental authority
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe humor - Ross reducing his role to biological function

jokes.text: Now imagine your vagina is opening... like a flower.
jokes.setup: Lamaze breathing exercise instruction
jokes.punchline: Uncomfortably graphic metaphor in clinical setting
jokes.explanation: Shock humor from medical instruction - relies on discomfort more than craft

jokes.text: We haven't seen an ANUS this bad since the '70s.
jokes.setup: Discussing Annual Net Usage Statistics
jokes.punchline: Another unfortunate acronym (ANUS)
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of office acronym humor - ANUS worse than WENUS

jokes.text: I slept funny and my hair was snarly.
jokes.setup: Chandler panicking about being fired
jokes.punchline: Defending tardiness with childish excuse
jokes.explanation: Chandler's panic leads to ridiculous professional excuse

jokes.text: Please don't hate me. / What? / Would you like to have dinner sometime?
jokes.setup: Chandler appears to be firing Nina
jokes.punchline: Actually asking her on a date instead
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - audience expects firing, gets romantic advance

jokes.text: Well, what I really want is for my mom to be alive and enjoy it with me.
jokes.setup: Asked what she wants for her birthday
jokes.punchline: Devastatingly honest answer about dead mother
jokes.explanation: Dark Phoebe humor - tragic honesty in casual context

jokes.text: Anything from Crabtree & Evelyn? / Bath salts would be nice.
jokes.setup: Monica pivoting from dead mother comment
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's practical response to gift suggestion
jokes.explanation: Good recovery beat - Phoebe moving from tragic to practical

jokes.text: I think we have an answer.
jokes.setup: Chandler asks 'How bad could it be?' about restaurant
jokes.punchline: Monica's response seeing the awful place
jokes.explanation: Standard setup/payoff - restaurant is visibly terrible

jokes.text: This could be God's way of telling us to eat at home.
jokes.setup: Seeing Ursula working at this restaurant too
jokes.punchline: Divine intervention interpretation of coincidence
jokes.explanation: Chandler's dramatic interpretation of awkward situation

jokes.text: Hi, it's us. / Right. And it's me.
jokes.setup: Monica trying to establish recognition
jokes.punchline: Ursula's matter-of-fact non-recognition
jokes.explanation: Ursula's deadpan response to attempted familiarity

jokes.text: So you're here too? / As much as you are. / Your turn.
jokes.setup: Monica stating obvious about Ursula's presence
jokes.punchline: Ursula's philosophical response and conversation direction
jokes.explanation: Ursula's zen-like deflection of small talk - unexpectedly profound

jokes.text: Congratulations. I think you found the world's thinnest argument.
jokes.setup: Chandler comparing not firing Nina to Christmas lights
jokes.punchline: Monica's assessment of his weak comparison
jokes.explanation: Great escalation language - 'world's thinnest' is perfect hyperbole

jokes.text: Sweetheart, you're fired. But how about a quickie before I go to work?
jokes.setup: Discussing Chandler's impossible dating/firing situation
jokes.punchline: Monica's crude imagined scenario
jokes.explanation: Crass but effective illustration of Chandler's dilemma

jokes.text: Once you're inside, you don't have to knock anymore.
jokes.setup: Joey knocking on door from inside apartment
jokes.punchline: Chandler's logical explanation of door etiquette
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about Joey's confusion

jokes.text: You don't even have cats. / I could have cats.
jokes.setup: Mr. Heckles complaining about cats' sleep
jokes.punchline: Defending hypothetical pet ownership
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mr. Heckles logic - potential reality justifies complaint

jokes.text: Oh, no, no. It's for Ursula. I just figured, you know, size-wise.
jokes.setup: Phoebe excited about receiving first birthday present
jokes.punchline: Joey reveals it's actually for her twin sister
jokes.explanation: Painful Joey insensitivity - using Phoebe as fitting model

jokes.text: I've never known you to pay money for any kind of capade.
jokes.setup: Joey taking Ursula to Ice Capades
jokes.punchline: Chandler's made-up word 'capade' as category
jokes.explanation: Brilliant word creation - 'capade' as suffix for entertainment shows

jokes.text: Well, maybe now I'm not okay with it not being okay.
jokes.setup: Phoebe changing her mind about Joey dating Ursula
jokes.punchline: Joey's confusing double-negative response
jokes.explanation: Joey's linguistic confusion creating accidentally complex statement

jokes.text: Knit, good woman. Knit. Knit.
jokes.setup: Phoebe angry and stress-knitting
jokes.punchline: Commanding herself to knit like a mantra
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Phoebe's hippie self-coaching through anger

jokes.text: Dr. Flanen... Dr. Flanan, Dr. Flan.
jokes.setup: Chandler making up psychiatrist's name
jokes.punchline: Name degrades to dessert as he panics
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Chandler's lie falling apart in real-time

jokes.text: That's unbelievable. / And yet believable.
jokes.setup: Mr. Dee responding to Nina's supposed mental health crisis
jokes.punchline: Chandler's contradiction maintaining the fiction
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay showing Chandler digging deeper into lie

jokes.text: Well, I guess that's why they call it psychology, sir.
jokes.setup: Mr. Dee noting you can't know what's in someone's head
jokes.punchline: Chandler's fake-profound response to obvious statement
jokes.explanation: Chandler's nervous attempt to sound wise while lying

jokes.text: It'll have to stay in, that's all. Everything will be the same. It'll just stay in.
jokes.setup: Carol seeing birth video
jokes.punchline: Deciding baby should never be born
jokes.explanation: Relatable pregnancy fear taken to absurd logical conclusion

jokes.text: Hi, is that your nostril? Mind if we push this pot roast through it?
jokes.setup: Carol explaining what birth feels like
jokes.punchline: Grotesque but accurate analogy for delivery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visceral analogy making birth relatable through food metaphor

jokes.text: I just never realized the baby was having me.
jokes.setup: Ross realizing he's going to be a father
jokes.punchline: Philosophical reversal of who's in control
jokes.explanation: Profound realization about parenthood - baby changes parent more than reverse

jokes.text: You know, Ross, some scientists are now saying that monkeys and babies are actually different.
jokes.setup: Ross worried about raising kid because he can't control Marcel
jokes.punchline: Susan's mock-scientific distinction
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan callback to monkey subplot with fake scientific authority

jokes.text: You meant sex, right?
jokes.setup: Joey asking if Monica and Chandler 'you know'
jokes.punchline: Clarifying his euphemistic question
jokes.explanation: Joey's direct approach to indirect questions - breaks fourth wall

jokes.text: But that doesn't explain why they keep taking my scissors.
jokes.setup: Nina noticing strange workplace behavior
jokes.punchline: Ominous detail suggesting she's considered dangerous
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation - office treating Nina as potential threat

jokes.text: I am? / Sure. Why not?
jokes.setup: Nina surprised about getting raise
jokes.punchline: Chandler's casual attitude toward major financial decision
jokes.explanation: Chandler's desperation leading to irresponsible business decisions

jokes.text: What? / Helen drinks.
jokes.setup: Helen mentions psychological profile over intercom
jokes.punchline: Chandler's quick cover story about secretary
jokes.explanation: Quick thinking deflection - blaming assistant for professional mistake

jokes.text: Never, ever leave your hand on the desk.
jokes.setup: Chandler describing telling Nina the truth
jokes.punchline: Warning about stapler violence
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy aftermath - office supplies as weapons

jokes.text: When I was 8, I wouldn't let her have my Judy Jetson Thermos... so she threw it under the bus.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining Ursula's destructiveness
jokes.punchline: Literal destruction of childhood possession
jokes.explanation: Specific childhood trauma with good visual detail

jokes.text: I mean, I know Joey's not my boyfriend or my Thermos... or anything, but...
jokes.setup: Phoebe worried about losing Joey to Ursula
jokes.punchline: Including Thermos in list of things Joey isn't
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback connecting Joey to childhood trauma through absurd comparison

jokes.text: Okay. Oh. Okay. Oh.
jokes.setup: Friends discussing Joey and Ursula's relationship
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's dawning realization about their intimacy
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy beat - Phoebe processing uncomfortable information

jokes.text: No, no, no. You wanted me to take them down... so I'm taking them down, okay?
jokes.setup: Monica telling Rachel to come inside from the cold
jokes.punchline: Rachel's stubborn determination to finish task
jokes.explanation: Rachel's pride preventing sensible decision - character consistency

jokes.text: See, this is just the kind of thing I was talking about.
jokes.setup: Rachel falls and yells while taking down lights
jokes.punchline: Mr. Heckles vindicated about noise complaints
jokes.explanation: Perfect vindication for cranky neighbor - his complaints proven justified
